items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel: tooling replaces LLM outputs that are not valid positioning_tag ids so ingest can continue.

items.label: Interdisciplinary
items.description: Positioning emphasizes cross-disciplinary work.

items.label: STEM-adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to engineering, computing, or sciences.

items.label: Fine Arts Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to the fine arts.

items.label: Architecture Adjacent
items.description: Emphasizes work adjacent to architecture.

items.label: Professional
items.description: Emphasizes industry readiness, studios, and professional skills.

items.label: Research
items.description: Emphasizes academic research trajectories and scholarly output.

items.label: Social Impact
items.description: Emphasizes public interest, equity, sustainability, or civic contexts.

items.label: Physical Making
items.description: Emphasizes physical making, fabrication, or production.

items.label: Digital
items.description: Emphasizes digital products, services, platforms, or apps.

items.label: UI/UX
items.description: Emphasizes user interface and user experience design.

items.label: Emerging Technologies
items.description: explicitly emphasizes emerging technologies.

items.label: Human Computer Interaction
items.description: explicitly emphasizes human computer interaction.

items.label: Entrepreneurship
items.description: explicitly emphasizes entrepreneurship.

items.label: Critial / Speculative Design
items.description: explicitly emphasizes critical or speculative design.

items.label: Interaction Design
items.description: explicitly emphasizes interaction design.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel: tooling replaces LLM outputs that are not valid course_type ids so ingest can continue.

items.label: Design studio
items.description: A project-based studio course that normally carries larger curricular weight than other courses.
rules: Use only when the course is clearly a project-based studio.
Do not classify a course as design_studio merely because it includes a project.

Use design_studio only when normalized_unit_weight is strictly greater than 1.1; at 1.1 or below (or when weight is unknown), use design_methods_systems instead. Higher weight above 1.1 should increase the likelihood of choosing design_studio when the course is clearly a heavy studio/project.
Design studio can never be hybrid.
If primary_type is design_studio, secondary_type must be null.

items.label: Human factors
items.description: Courses focused on people, communities, users, behavior, culture, social context, design research, ethnography, ergonomics, or human-centered design.

items.label: Technology
items.description: Courses focused on technical design methods, computational tools, software, hardware, fabrication, sensors, AI, robotics, CAD, optimization, or technologies used in the design itself.

items.label: History, theory, and positioning
items.description: Courses focused on design history, design theory, critical theory, contemporary issues, ethics, politics, movements, disciplinary framing, or colloquia.

items.label: Rhetoric, storytelling, and presentation
items.description: Courses focused on writing, presenting, communicating, documenting, exhibiting, pitching, or narrating design work.

items.label: Design methods and systems
items.description: Courses focused on design process, structured methods, systems thinking, strategy, problem framing, research synthesis, service design methods, participatory design, systems mapping, or design frameworks.

items.label: Entrepreneurship
items.description: Courses focused on venture creation, innovation management, commercialization, markets, business models, product-market fit, leadership, startup practice, or go-to-market strategy.

items.label: Open or other
items.description: Use when a course or elective slot is broad, unspecified, intentionally flexible, or does not clearly map to one of the defined types.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid host_academic_model ids.

items.label: co-hosted by engineering + design units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both engineering and design units.

items.label: co-hosted by engineering + fine arts units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both engineering and arts units.

items.label: co-hosted by engineering + business units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both engineering and business units.

items.label: co-hosted by design + business units
items.description: Program is administratively housed in both design and business units.

items.label: hosted in an engineering unit
items.description: Administered by an engineering school or college.

items.label: hosted in a design unit
items.description: Administered by a design school or college.

items.label: hosted in an arts / humanities units
items.description: Administered by a unit in arts, humanities, or fine arts.

items.label: hosted in an business / management unit
items.description: Administered by a unit in business or management.

items.label: hosted in an CS / information technology unit
items.description: Administered by a unit in computer science or information technology.

items.label: Unknown
items.description: Academic unit is not clearly associated with any known categories.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid duration_category ids.

items.label: Less than 2 semesters
items.description: Program length is less than two standard academic semesters.

items.label: Two semesters
items.description: Roughly two standard academic semesters.

items.label: Three semesters
items.description: Roughly three standard academic semesters.

items.label: Four semesters
items.description: Roughly four standard academic semesters.

items.label: Four or more semesters
items.description: Extended programs spanning more than four standard academic semesters.

items.label: Variable or custom
items.description: Duration is student-paced, modular, or not expressed in standard terms.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid sequencedness ids.

items.label: Highly sequenced
items.description: A large majority of required curriculum items (especially core courses) have an official semester or term placement in degree-plan materials—not only a broad year label.
rules: Operational rule: among required courses (or plan rows) you can list from evidence, compute the share that name a **specific semester or term** (e.g. Fall Year 1, Spring Year 2). If **roughly one-half (50%) or more** have that level of placement, prefer **highly_sequenced**.

items.label: Partially sequenced
items.description: Meaningful sequencing (e.g. a prescribed first-year core or prerequisites) but a substantial portion of required or listed courses lack an explicit semester/term in the materials you have, or placement stops at year-level.
rules: If **fewer than ~two-thirds** of enumerated required items show a **specific term** (many are only "Year 1 core", placeholders, or undated catalog lines), prefer **partially_sequenced** over highly_sequenced.
Also use partially_sequenced when only **some** threads are term-mapped while others are intentionally open across terms.

items.label: Flexible
items.description: Minimal prescribed term-by-term sequencing for required work; students choose timing across terms within broad requirements.
rules: Use when evidence shows few or no required courses pinned to particular semesters, beyond generic credit progress rules.

items.label: Invalid / unknown
items.description: Reserved sentinel for values that are not valid unit_system ids.

items.label: Semester credit hours
items.description: US-style semester credits.

items.label: Quarter units
items.description: US-style quarter-system units.

items.label: ECTS
items.description: European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System.

items.label: Other or mixed
items.description: Nonstandard or explicitly mixed unit systems.
